memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-05-17 — Weekend Analysis

- Trump-Xi Summit Day 2 (May 15) = 'Chip Letdown': NVDA H200 cleared for Chinese hyperscalers; AMD MI308 NOT cleared. SOX -5-6.8%. S&P -1.24%.
- AMD re-scored: 7.33→7.0 avg; Sentiment 7→6 (AMD-specific China disappointment), Macro 7→6 (risk-off). Only 3/6 agents ≥7 — fails 4/6 Master Agent requirement. REJECTED. Not deployment bias violation — legitimate re-score on changed conditions.
- MU scored 7.33 (5/6 agents ≥7). Post-summit selloff to $732.49 creates better entry than planned $788. HBM4 supply sold out for 2026 — thesis entirely US/TSMC-customer driven, NOT China-dependent. APPROVED for Monday MOO (6sh).
- NVDA earnings CORRECTED to May 20 AH (not May 21). 48h binary event exclusion from Monday open.
- Portfolio gap vs SPX improved to -2.52 pp from -3.81 pp (portfolio fell less due to 81% cash).

### 2026-05-15 — Summit Day 2 & Chip Letdown

- AMD MI308 not cleared in China deal. Fundamental AI thesis intact (US data center) but China revenue upside thesis invalidated.
- XLE +2.4% on oil/Hormuz. GLD -2.3% (risk-off surprise). BTC $78K (below $82K).
- 10-year yield 4.55% (1-year high) = tech/growth headwind, especially JPM.

market.macro_notes_md:
- **Trump-Xi Summit Day 2 (May 15):** No formal semiconductor deal. NVDA H200 cleared for 4 Chinese co's; AMD MI308 NOT cleared. 'Chip letdown' — SOX -5-6.8%.
- **NVDA Earnings May 20 AH (CORRECTED):** Consensus Rev $78-80B, EPS $1.76-1.79. Strong Buy consensus, avg PT $273.62. 48-hour binary event exclusion from Monday May 18 open. No new NVDA entries.
- **Iran/Oil:** US-Iran deal stalled. Hormuz restricted. WTI $103-104/bbl. XLE benefiting. GLD surprisingly soft.
- **10-year yield:** 4.55% (1-year high). Fed on hold. Growth/tech headwind. JPM NIM headwind.
- **Bitcoin:** $78,082 (May 15). Below $82K threshold. No summit crypto language.
- **SOX warning:** 32% above 50-day MA per Schwab — one of widest historical premiums. Consolidation risk for chip sector.
- **Week of May 18 key events:** NVDA earnings May 20 AH; FOMC minutes May 20; HD/TOL/CAVA earnings May 19.
